canadian_markets.title: S&P/TSX edges lower as oil retreats from wartime highs, energy weighs
canadian_markets.summary: Canada's main stock index slipped 0.06% to 33,944, dragged by energy names as crude pulled back sharply from the $126/bbl peak touched Thursday.

canadian_markets.title: Bank of Canada warns of 'consecutive hikes' if oil keeps pushing inflation higher
canadian_markets.summary: Governor Tiff Macklem held at 2.25% for the fourth straight meeting but warned: if elevated oil prices feed into broader inflation, there may be a need for consecutive increases.

us_global_markets.title: S&P 500 smashes through 7,270 — new all-time high as Mag 7 earnings deliver
us_global_markets.summary: The S&P 500 closed at a record 7,267.20 (+0.8%), touching an intraday all-time high of 7,272.52. Apple's Q2 — $111.2B revenue, +17% YoY — capped a week where Mag 7 earnings shattered expectations.

us_global_markets.title: Fed holds at 3.50-3.75% in Powell's final meeting; Warsh confirmation advances
us_global_markets.summary: The Fed kept rates steady. Powell confirmed he departs as chair May 15. Kevin Warsh's nomination cleared a key Senate committee after Sen. Tillis lifted his block.

prediction_markets.market: Powell departs as Fed Chair by May 16

analysis.theme: Markets are threading the needle — pricing both an AI-driven earnings boom and persistent geopolitical oil risk.
analysis.cad_angle: The loonie is caught in a tug-of-war: oil revenues pad the current account, but US-Canada trade uncertainty caps upside.
analysis.risk_factor: If oil-driven inflation broadens beyond energy, the BoC could be forced into a hiking cycle that cools housing just as mortgage renewals peak.
analysis.polymarket_signal: The 68.5% BoC hike probability is the dominant macro trade — markets believe oil stays elevated long enough to force the Bank's hand.

long_term.label: No new trade deal
long_term.impact: CAD under pressure · Exporters face uncertainty

long_term.label: Canada recession before 2027
long_term.impact: TSX -10-15% · CAD -2-3% · BoC forced to cut

scenarios.key_observation: The market is simultaneously pricing a 68.5% BoC hike and a 47% Iran de-escalation. These cannot both materialize. If de-escalation wins, oil drops and the hiking thesis collapses. If escalation wins, Macklem's warning becomes a near-certainty.
